source.title: Motor Vessel Allision with South Pass 27 #5 Platform

context.region: Gulf of America, South Pass Block 27

actions_taken: MV crew assessed damage to vessel and platform
MV notified United States Coast Guard
MV dropped off Island Operators personnel at WD 106 platform
MV proceeded to Allied Shipyard for repairs
Safety alert to be shared with GOL fleet
Navigation policy review scheduled for GOL fleet
event.summary: On 18 July 2025 at 0730 hours, Motor Vessel Marc C allided with South Pass 27 #5 platform during platform surveying operations for decommissioning. The vessel captain, after completing surveys in the East Bay field and setting course to West Delta field, became distracted by computer work and failed to maintain proper lookout. The captain did not use the electronic navigation chart system that clearly identified platform structures. The allision resulted in damage to both the platform and vessel starboard bow, with no injuries or pollution.
recommendations: Issue Safety Alert regarding vessel navigation near platforms and structures
Review and reinforce safe navigation and voyage planning policy with GOL fleet
Implement proper lookout procedures when captain is performing administrative tasks
Ensure use of electronic navigation chart systems during voyage planning and transit
key_phrases: failure to use electronic navigation chart

controls.name: Electronic Navigation Chart System
controls.barrier_role: Identify platform structures and navigational hazards
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent collision by providing real-time structure identification

supporting_text: The MV captain did not use his electronic navigation chart system that has the structures clearly identified
This is a violation of the company safe navigation/voyage planning policy

controls.name: Safe Navigation and Voyage Planning Policy
controls.barrier_role: Establish procedures for safe vessel navigation and course setting
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control requiring captain to clear all structures before setting course

supporting_text: The captain violated company safe navigations/voyage planning policy by setting his course to the WD field prior to clearing all structures in the East Bay field
The captains disregard for GOL's navigation policy, and focusing his attention on his computer rather than navigating the vessel while underway, caused the allision

controls.name: Vessel Lookout Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Maintain continuous visual observation of vessel heading and surrounding structures
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to detect navigational hazards through human observation

supporting_text: The captain allowing himself to become distracted by working on the computer and not designating proper look-out personnel while he did his computer work
No witnesses on board the MV at the time of the incident saw the MV contact the platform
One witness stated that they were in the wheelhouse with the captain at the time of the incident but had their back turned away from the bow of the MV

controls.name: Captain Situational Awareness and Task Focus
controls.barrier_role: Maintain attention on vessel navigation and heading
controls.lod_basis: Primary human-based control requiring captain to focus on navigation task

supporting_text: The captain then turned around to do paperwork on his computer. The impact with SP 27 #5 was approximately 3 minutes after he set his course
The boat captain was not paying attention to the MV's heading once he set course to the WD field and turned his attention to performing computer tasks
Due to the lack of awareness by the boat captain and the failure to use the proper electronic navigation chart system, the MV contacted the SP 27 #5 platform

controls.name: Platform Structure Visibility and Marking
controls.barrier_role: Ensure platform structures are visible to approaching vessels
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to mitigate collision consequence through improved visibility

supporting_text: The platform's low profile which could have caused limited visibility
The boat captain could have experienced platform visibility issues due to the MV wheelhouse area sitting much higher than the low-lying structure

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
